raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding the handling of a billing dispute by American Express concerning a charge of {$1000.00} from a rental car company XXXX in XXXX / XXXX dated XX/XX/year> ( Case number XXXX ). 

I disputed this charge because the merchant failed to provide the vehicle that was specifically reserved and confirmed prior to my international travel. I had reserved a XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX and confirmed its by phone XXXX hours before arrival. I spoke directly with the rental location at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX before our arrival. I was assured the vehicle would be available. Although it is written on the contract or similar vehicle. I wanted to know what car is available for us. They told me the car is waiting for you and they confirmed that the XXXX is available and ready for us. 
Upon arrival, I was informed that the reserved vehicle was not available. Instead, I was given a XXXX XXXX a XXXX XXXXXXXX. I did not want that car but had no choice, nothing else was available. Because the vehicle was classified by the merchant as a commercial vehicle, the prepaid insurance coverage I had purchased in advance was not honored. I was required to purchase additional insurance in order to receive any vehicle and put a large deposit triple the amount due to its class. 
After arriving XXXX, XXXX flights, my family and luggage, I had no reasonable alternative but to accept the vehicle and the additional insurance charge in order to continue our travel. we were on a way to a wedding and family reunion. 
I used my American Express card with the knowledge that I can always dispute and I am safe with them. 
On my return to the XXXX, I contacted AMEX and provided all the documents, letter with explanation. Despite this documentation, American Express denied my dispute based solely on documents signed at the rental counter ( which I had no choice ). Those documents were signed only as part of the rental process under circumstances where no reasonable alternative transportation was available. I did not want to sign but they did not want to give me a car without the signature. 

The additional insurance charge resulted directly from the merchants failure to provide the contracted passenger vehicle and the refusal to honor prepaid coverage. 

I am requesting that American Express conduct a fair review of the dispute and reconsider the decision.
